title: Fetch ('F')
html:
Fetch ('F')

Add to My Topics
Add selected topic and subtopicsAdd Topic & Subtopics with topic headAdd entire publication
Watch
Download PDF
Save selected topicSave selected topic and subtopicsSave entire publication
Share
Email LinkCopy Link
Feedback
  • Last UpdatedJuli 17, 2024
    • Uniface
    • 10.4

Fetch a record from a table or lock a record.

Description

Both the if list and the first entry in the where structure identify exactly one record. The record can be identified by either the primary key or the PRA. If the DBMS is capable of performing the selection specified in the if list, use the if list to select the record. If the DBMS is ISAM-like, use the first entry in the where structure.

Table 1. Fetch Modes
Mode Description
Mode 0 Fetch the specified record.

Update the entry of the record in the hitlist by means of the uaddhit service function.

If the DBMS supports PRAs and the field uctrl->urdbykey is 0, Fetch the PRA with the record and add it to the hitlist as well.
Mode 1 Fetch the specified record and lock it exclusively so that from this moment it is guaranteed that the record can be updated or deleted in this transaction. Except for the lock, mode 1 is identical to mode 0.
Mode 2 This mode needs to be implemented only for ISAM-like DBMSs. For SQL-like DBMSs, return 0 to Uniface.

Lock the specified record without fetching any data. Choose the same locking type as in mode 1.

Position on the record even if the DBMS does not support locking. The position can be used to identify the record in the next call to the connector (which will be an Update or a Delete).

If the DBMS does not support locking and no errors occurred with the positioning, return 0 to Uniface.
Mode 3 Reserved for future use.

From Uniface

Preconditions realized by Uniface—The table that the Fetch is requested for is open.

Table 2. Information Available from Uniface
Data Structure->Element Description
uctrl->TMEnabled Indicates whether the connector is TM-controlled.
uctrl->udrv Points to the connector workspace if a workspace was requested in the Info call.
uctrl->udrvini Unique identification of the current logon path.
uctrl->ufldlst The field list which describes all the fields in the table.
uctrl->uwlist First entry describes the index to be used for ISAM-like connectors. For field-level connectors, the field uctrl->uwlist->ufnr can be used to determine if the record is identified by PRA (ufnr equals 0), or by the primary key (ufnr equals -1) without having to scan the uiflst structure.
uctrl->uiflst Identifies the record to be deleted (SQL-based DBMSs only).
uctrl->iobuf Points to the general I/O buffer.
uctrl->sqlbuf Points to the SQL buffer.
uctrl->uilist Points to the index list.
uctrl->vsfnr If vsfnr equals 0, this indicates that segmented fields do not have to be fetched, otherwise they do have to be fetched. When the request concerned is a Fetch lock (mode 1) or Fetch position (mode 2), nonzero vsfnr will be 0.

To Uniface

The fetched record must be stored in the general I/O buffer. For every field, the offset in this buffer is available in ufldlst->ufpos. The connector is responsible for storing the fields at the correct positions.

For field-level DBMSs, the actual length of the fetched value of every field must be stored in the field uflen in the field list. For both field-level and record-level DBMSs, the total length of the fetched record must be stored in uctrl->udatlen. The total length is defined as the position of the last field (ufpos), plus its actual length (uflen).

If the connector maintains the field uctrl->ulock, this field must be set to a nonzero value when a Fetch mode 1 or 2 has succeeded, thus showing the record has been locked.

Table 3. Connector Request Return Values
Value Description
0 Fetch or lock was successful.
-2 Specified record does not exist.
-3 Exceptional error.
-9 I/O server logon error.
-11 Specified record could not be fetched or locked due to a lock conflict.
-16 DBMS reported network error.

When the connector request return value is less than 0, an error code must be supplied in uctrl->udrverr.

Service Functions

uaddhit— Add a record to the hitlist.

Extra information

If all fields in the primary key are available, Uniface may call the Fetch request without a previous Select. When the select cache is used, records can be fetched without a Fetch request.

Uniface supports three different locking strategies: optimistic, cautious and paranoid. The locking strategy determines which action triggers a lock, but it does not change the locking type. The connector need not be aware of which locking strategy is being used.

When a Fetch returns -11, Uniface immediately reports the lock conflict to the end user. If a retry or pending mechanism with time-out is needed, this must be implemented in the connector.

If the DBMS supports different locking granularities (such as field locking, record locking, physical page locking or table locking), lock a single record if possible, or the next larger unit if necessary.

When the connector has to fetch segmented fields (vsfnr is not equal to 0) the segments of the segmented field have to be passed directly to the Uniface kernel using the uputblob service function.

TM-Controlled Connectors

TM-controlled DBMS connectors are not allowed to begin, commit, or rollback transactions.

Related Links
  • uctrl

    Exchanges general information between Uniface and the connector, and describes a table.

  • TM-Controlled DBMS Connectors

    A database connector can be controlled by a transaction manager only if it is TM-capable. Uniface does not provide TM-controlled connectors and does not directly support third-party transaction managers. However, you can use the APIs provided by the Database Connector Interface to build a custom database connector that does.

  • uputblob

    Used during Fetch to send the segmented data to the Uniface kernel.

Was this topic helpful?
